**Ngày 211-213: Microservices Implementation**
```java
// 1. SERVICE DISCOVERY với Eureka

// application.yml for Eureka Server

  port: 8761

    hostname: localhost

// Microservice Client Configuration
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableEurekaClient
@EnableFeignClients
public class BlogServiceApplication {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SpringApplication.run(BlogServiceApplication.class, args);
    }
}

// 2. API GATEWAY với Spring Cloud Gateway
@Configuration
@EnableWebFluxSecurity
public class GatewayConfig {

    @Bean
    public RouteLocator customRouteLocator(RouteLocatorBuilder builder) {
        return builder.routes()
            // Blog Service Routes
            .route("blog-service", r -> r
                .path("/api/blogs/**")
                .filters(f -> f
                    .circuitBreaker(config -> config
                        .setName("blog-service-cb")
                        .setFallbackUri("forward:/fallback/blogs"))
                    .retry(retryConfig -> retryConfig
                        .setRetries(3)
                        .setBackoff(Duration.ofMillis(100), Duration.ofMillis(1000), 2, true))
                    .requestRateLimiter(config -> config
                        .setRateLimiter(redisRateLimiter())
                        .setKeyResolver(userKeyResolver())))
                .uri("lb://blog-service"))

            // User Service Routes
            .route("user-service", r -> r
                .path("/api/users/**")
                .filters(f -> f
                    .circuitBreaker(config -> config
                        .setName("user-service-cb")
                        .setFallbackUri("forward:/fallback/users"))
                    .addRequestHeader("X-Gateway", "Spring-Cloud-Gateway"))
                .uri("lb://user-service"))

            // File Service Routes
            .route("file-service", r -> r
                .path("/api/files/**")
                .filters(f -> f
                    .circuitBreaker(config -> config
                        .setName("file-service-cb"))
                    .requestSize(10000000L)) // 10MB limit
                .uri("lb://file-service"))

            .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public RedisRateLimiter redisRateLimiter() {
        return new RedisRateLimiter(10, 20, 1); // 10 requests per second, burst of 20
    }

    @Bean
    public KeyResolver userKeyResolver() {
        return exchange -> exchange.getPrincipal()
            .cast(JwtAuthenticationToken.class)
            .map(JwtAuthenticationToken::getToken)
            .map(jwt -> jwt.getClaimAsString("sub"))
            .switchIfEmpty(Mono.just("anonymous"));
    }
}

// 3. CIRCUIT BREAKER với Resilience4j
@Component
public class UserServiceClient {

    private final WebClient webClient;
    private final CircuitBreaker circuitBreaker;
    private final Retry retry;
    private final TimeLimiter timeLimiter;

    public UserServiceClient(WebClient.Builder webClientBuilder,
                           CircuitBreakerRegistry circuitBreakerRegistry,
                           RetryRegistry retryRegistry,
                           TimeLimiterRegistry timeLimiterRegistry) {
        this.webClient = webClientBuilder.baseUrl("http://user-service").build();
        this.circuitBreaker = circuitBreakerRegistry.circuitBreaker("user-service");
        this.retry = retryRegistry.retry("user-service");
        this.timeLimiter = timeLimiterRegistry.timeLimiter("user-service");
    }

    public Mono<User> getUser(Long userId) {
        Supplier<CompletableFuture<User>> futureSupplier = () ->
            webClient.get()
                .uri("/api/users/{id}", userId)
                .retrieve()
                .bodyToMono(User.class)
                .toFuture();

        return Mono.fromCompletionStage(
            timeLimiter.executeCompletionStage(
                circuitBreaker.executeCompletionStage(
                    retry.executeCompletionStage(futureSupplier)
                )
            )
        ).onErrorResume(this::handleUserServiceError);
    }

    private Mono<User> handleUserServiceError(Throwable throwable) {
        log.error("Error calling user service", throwable);

        // Return default user or cached data
        return Mono.just(User.builder()
            .id(-1L)
            .name("Unknown User")
            .email("unknown@example.com")
            .build());
    }
}

@RefreshScope
public class ConfigController {

    @Value("${app.feature.new-ui:false}")
    private boolean newUiEnabled;

    @Value("${app.cache.ttl:3600}")
    private int cacheTtl;

    @GetMapping("/config")

// 5. DISTRIBUTED TRACING với Zipkin
@Configuration
public class TracingConfig {

    @Bean
    public Sender sender() {
        return OkHttpSender.create("http://zipkin:9411/api/v2/spans");
    }

    @Bean
    public AsyncReporter<Span> spanReporter() {
        return AsyncReporter.create(sender());
    }

    @Bean
    public Tracing tracing() {
        return Tracing.newBuilder()
            .localServiceName("blog-service")
            .spanReporter(spanReporter())
            .sampler(Sampler.create(1.0f)) // Sample 100% in development
            .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public HttpTracing httpTracing(Tracing tracing) {
        return HttpTracing.create(tracing);
    }
}

@Service
public class BlogService {

    @Autowired
    private BlogRepository blogRepository;

    @Autowired
    private UserServiceClient userServiceClient;

    @NewSpan("blog-service.create-blog")
    public Mono<Blog> createBlog(@SpanTag("blog.title") String title,
                                @SpanTag("user.id") Long userId) {
        return userServiceClient.getUser(userId)
            .flatMap(user -> {
                Blog blog = Blog.builder()
                    .title(title)
                    .authorId(userId)
                    .createdAt(LocalDateTime.now())
                    .build();

                return Mono.fromCallable(() -> blogRepository.save(blog))
                    .subscribeOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic());
            });
    }
}

// 6. MESSAGE QUEUES với RabbitMQ
@Configuration
@EnableRabbit
public class RabbitConfig {

    public static final String BLOG_EXCHANGE = "blog.exchange";
    public static final String BLOG_CREATED_QUEUE = "blog.created.queue";
    public static final String BLOG_UPDATED_QUEUE = "blog.updated.queue";
    public static final String BLOG_DELETED_QUEUE = "blog.deleted.queue";

    @Bean
    public TopicExchange blogExchange() {
        return new TopicExchange(BLOG_EXCHANGE);
    }

    @Bean
    public Queue blogCreatedQueue() {
        return QueueBuilder.durable(BLOG_CREATED_QUEUE)
            .withArgument("x-dead-letter-exchange", "dlx.exchange")
            .withArgument("x-message-ttl", 60000)
            .build();
    }

    @Bean
    public Queue blogUpdatedQueue() {
        return QueueBuilder.durable(BLOG_UPDATED_QUEUE).build();
    }

    @Bean
    public Queue blogDeletedQueue() {
        return QueueBuilder.durable(BLOG_DELETED_QUEUE).build();
    }

    @Bean
    public Binding blogCreatedBinding() {
        return BindingBuilder
            .bind(blogCreatedQueue())
            .to(blogExchange())
            .with("blog.created");
    }

    @Bean
    public Binding blogUpdatedBinding() {
        return BindingBuilder
            .bind(blogUpdatedQueue())
            .to(blogExchange())
            .with("blog.updated");
    }

    @Bean
    public Binding blogDeletedBinding() {
        return BindingBuilder
            .bind(blogDeletedQueue())
            .to(blogExchange())
            .with("blog.deleted");
    }

    @Bean
    public RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate(ConnectionFactory connectionFactory) {
        RabbitTemplate template = new RabbitTemplate(connectionFactory);
        template.setMessageConverter(new Jackson2JsonMessageConverter());
        template.setConfirmCallback((correlationData, ack, cause) -> {
            if (!ack) {
                log.error("Message not delivered: {}", cause);
            }
        });
        return template;
    }
}

// Event Publisher
@Service
public class BlogEventPublisher {

    @Autowired
    private RabbitTemplate rabbitTemplate;

    public void publishBlogCreated(Blog blog) {
        BlogCreatedEvent event = BlogCreatedEvent.builder()
            .blogId(blog.getId())
            .title(blog.getTitle())
            .authorId(blog.getAuthorId())
            .createdAt(blog.getCreatedAt())
            .build();

        rabbitTemplate.convertAndSend(
            RabbitConfig.BLOG_EXCHANGE,
            "blog.created",
            event
        );

        log.info("Published blog created event: {}", event);
    }

    public void publishBlogUpdated(Blog blog) {
        BlogUpdatedEvent event = BlogUpdatedEvent.builder()
            .blogId(blog.getId())
            .title(blog.getTitle())
            .updatedAt(blog.getUpdatedAt())
            .build();

        rabbitTemplate.convertAndSend(
            RabbitConfig.BLOG_EXCHANGE,
            "blog.updated",
            event
        );
    }

    public void publishBlogDeleted(Long blogId) {
        BlogDeletedEvent event = BlogDeletedEvent.builder()
            .blogId(blogId)
            .deletedAt(LocalDateTime.now())
            .build();

        rabbitTemplate.convertAndSend(
            RabbitConfig.BLOG_EXCHANGE,
            "blog.deleted",
            event
        );
    }
}

// Event Listeners
@Component
public class BlogEventListener {

    @Autowired
    private SearchIndexService searchIndexService;

    @Autowired
    private CacheService cacheService;

    @Autowired
    private NotificationService notificationService;

    @RabbitListener(queues = RabbitConfig.BLOG_CREATED_QUEUE)
    public void handleBlogCreated(BlogCreatedEvent event) {
        try {
            // Update search index
            searchIndexService.indexBlog(event.getBlogId());

            // Clear cache
            cacheService.evictBlogCache();

            // Send notifications
            notificationService.notifySubscribers(event);

            log.info("Processed blog created event: {}", event);

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Error processing blog created event: {}", event, e);
            throw new AmqpRejectAndDontRequeueException("Failed to process event", e);
        }
    }

    @RabbitListener(queues = RabbitConfig.BLOG_UPDATED_QUEUE)
    public void handleBlogUpdated(BlogUpdatedEvent event) {
        try {
            // Update search index
            searchIndexService.updateBlogIndex(event.getBlogId());

            // Clear cache
            cacheService.evictBlogCache(event.getBlogId());

            log.info("Processed blog updated event: {}", event);

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Error processing blog updated event: {}", event, e);
            throw new AmqpRejectAndDontRequeueException("Failed to process event", e);
        }
    }

    @RabbitListener(queues = RabbitConfig.BLOG_DELETED_QUEUE)
    public void handleBlogDeleted(BlogDeletedEvent event) {
        try {
            // Remove from search index
            searchIndexService.removeBlogFromIndex(event.getBlogId());

            // Clear cache
            cacheService.evictBlogCache(event.getBlogId());

            log.info("Processed blog deleted event: {}", event);

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Error processing blog deleted event: {}", event, e);
            throw new AmqpRejectAndDontRequeueException("Failed to process event", e);
        }
    }
}

// 7. SAGA PATTERN for Distributed Transactions
@Component
public class BlogCreationSaga {

    @Autowired
    private BlogService blogService;

    @Autowired
    private UserService userService;

    @Autowired
    private NotificationService notificationService;

    @Autowired
    private SearchIndexService searchIndexService;

    @SagaOrchestrationStart
    public void createBlog(CreateBlogCommand command) {
        SagaTransaction transaction = SagaTransaction.builder()
            .sagaId(UUID.randomUUID().toString())
            .command(command)
            .build();

        try {
            // Step 1: Validate user
            validateUser(transaction);

            // Step 2: Create blog
            createBlogPost(transaction);

            // Step 3: Update search index
            updateSearchIndex(transaction);

            // Step 4: Send notifications
            sendNotifications(transaction);

            // Mark saga as completed
            transaction.setStatus(SagaStatus.COMPLETED);

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Saga failed, starting compensation", e);
            compensate(transaction);
        }
    }

    private void validateUser(SagaTransaction transaction) {
        CreateBlogCommand command = transaction.getCommand();

        if (!userService.userExists(command.getAuthorId())) {
            throw new SagaException("User does not exist: " + command.getAuthorId());
        }

        transaction.addCompensation(() -> {
            // No compensation needed for validation
        });
    }

    private void createBlogPost(SagaTransaction transaction) {
        CreateBlogCommand command = transaction.getCommand();

        Blog blog = blogService.createBlog(command.getTitle(), command.getContent(), command.getAuthorId());
        transaction.setBlogId(blog.getId());

        transaction.addCompensation(() -> {
            blogService.deleteBlog(blog.getId());
        });
    }

    private void updateSearchIndex(SagaTransaction transaction) {
        searchIndexService.indexBlog(transaction.getBlogId());

        transaction.addCompensation(() -> {
            searchIndexService.removeBlogFromIndex(transaction.getBlogId());
        });
    }

    private void sendNotifications(SagaTransaction transaction) {
        notificationService.notifyBlogCreated(transaction.getBlogId());

        transaction.addCompensation(() -> {
            // Notifications can't be "unsent", but we can send a cancellation notice
            notificationService.notifyBlogCreationCancelled(transaction.getBlogId());
        });
    }

    private void compensate(SagaTransaction transaction) {
        transaction.getCompensations().forEach(compensation -> {
            try {
                compensation.run();
            } catch (Exception e) {
                log.error("Compensation failed", e);
            }
        });

        transaction.setStatus(SagaStatus.COMPENSATED);
    }
}
```

**Ngày 214-217: Advanced Database & Event-Driven Architecture**
```java
// 1. DATABASE SHARDING Strategy
@Configuration
public class ShardingConfig {

    @Bean
    @Primary
    public DataSource shardedDataSource() {
        Map<Object, Object> dataSources = new HashMap<>();

        // Shard 0 - Users 0-999999
        dataSources.put("shard0", createDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-shard0:3306/blog_shard0"));

        // Shard 1 - Users 1000000-1999999
        dataSources.put("shard1", createDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-shard1:3306/blog_shard1"));

        // Shard 2 - Users 2000000+
        dataSources.put("shard2", createDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-shard2:3306/blog_shard2"));

        ShardingDataSource shardingDataSource = new ShardingDataSource();
        shardingDataSource.setTargetDataSources(dataSources);
        shardingDataSource.setDefaultTargetDataSource(dataSources.get("shard0"));

        return shardingDataSource;
    }

    private DataSource createDataSource(String url) {
        HikariConfig config = new HikariConfig();
        config.setJdbcUrl(url);
        config.setUsername("blog_user");
        config.setPassword("blog_password");
        config.setMaximumPoolSize(20);
        config.setMinimumIdle(5);
        config.setConnectionTimeout(30000);
        config.setIdleTimeout(600000);
        config.setMaxLifetime(1800000);

        return new HikariDataSource(config);
    }
}

@Component
public class ShardingStrategy {

    public String determineShardKey(Long userId) {
        if (userId < 1000000) {
            return "shard0";
        } else if (userId < 2000000) {
            return "shard1";
        } else {
            return "shard2";
        }
    }

    public String determineShardKeyByEmail(String email) {
        int hash = email.hashCode();
        int shardIndex = Math.abs(hash) % 3;
        return "shard" + shardIndex;
    }
}

// 2. READ/WRITE SPLITTING
@Configuration
public class ReadWriteSplittingConfig {

    @Bean
    @Primary
    public DataSource routingDataSource() {
        ReadWriteRoutingDataSource routingDataSource = new ReadWriteRoutingDataSource();

        Map<Object, Object> dataSources = new HashMap<>();
        dataSources.put("write", createWriteDataSource());
        dataSources.put("read1", createReadDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-read1:3306/blog"));
        dataSources.put("read2", createReadDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-read2:3306/blog"));

        routingDataSource.setTargetDataSources(dataSources);
        routingDataSource.setDefaultTargetDataSource(dataSources.get("write"));

        return routingDataSource;
    }

    private DataSource createWriteDataSource() {
        return createDataSource("jdbc:mysql://db-write:3306/blog");
    }

    private DataSource createReadDataSource(String url) {
        return createDataSource(url);
    }
}

public class ReadWriteRoutingDataSource extends AbstractRoutingDataSource {

    @Override
    protected Object determineCurrentLookupKey() {
        String dataSourceType = DataSourceContextHolder.getDataSourceType();

        if ("read".equals(dataSourceType)) {
            // Load balance between read replicas
            List<String> readDataSources = Arrays.asList("read1", "read2");
            int index = ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(readDataSources.size());
            return readDataSources.get(index);
        }

        return "write";
    }
}

// 3. EVENT SOURCING Implementation
@Entity
@Table(name = "event_store")
public class EventStore {

    @Id
    private String eventId;

    @Column(name = "aggregate_id")
    private String aggregateId;

    @Column(name = "aggregate_type")
    private String aggregateType;

    @Column(name = "event_type")
    private String eventType;

    @Column(name = "event_data", columnDefinition = "JSON")
    private String eventData;

    @Column(name = "event_version")
    private Long eventVersion;

    @Column(name = "created_at")
    private LocalDateTime createdAt;

    // Getters and setters
}

@Service
public class EventSourcingService {

    @Autowired
    private EventStoreRepository eventStoreRepository;

    @Autowired
    private ObjectMapper objectMapper;

    public void saveEvent(DomainEvent event) {
        try {
            EventStore eventStore = EventStore.builder()
                .eventId(UUID.randomUUID().toString())
                .aggregateId(event.getAggregateId())
                .aggregateType(event.getAggregateType())
                .eventType(event.getClass().getSimpleName())
                .eventData(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(event))
                .eventVersion(event.getVersion())
                .createdAt(LocalDateTime.now())
                .build();

            eventStoreRepository.save(eventStore);

        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new EventSourcingException("Failed to save event", e);
        }
    }

    public List<DomainEvent> getEvents(String aggregateId) {
        List<EventStore> eventStores = eventStoreRepository
            .findByAggregateIdOrderByEventVersionAsc(aggregateId);

        return eventStores.stream()
            .map(this::deserializeEvent)
            .collect(Collectors.toList());
    }

    public <T> T rebuildAggregate(String aggregateId, Class<T> aggregateClass) {
        List<DomainEvent> events = getEvents(aggregateId);

        try {
            T aggregate = aggregateClass.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance();

            for (DomainEvent event : events) {
                applyEvent(aggregate, event);
            }

            return aggregate;

        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new EventSourcingException("Failed to rebuild aggregate", e);
        }
    }

    private DomainEvent deserializeEvent(EventStore eventStore) {
        try {
            Class<?> eventClass = Class.forName("dev.cuonghoang.events." + eventStore.getEventType());
            return (DomainEvent) objectMapper.readValue(eventStore.getEventData(), eventClass);
        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new EventSourcingException("Failed to deserialize event", e);
        }
    }

    private void applyEvent(Object aggregate, DomainEvent event) {
        try {
            Method applyMethod = aggregate.getClass()
                .getDeclaredMethod("apply", event.getClass());
            applyMethod.setAccessible(true);
            applyMethod.invoke(aggregate, event);
        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new EventSourcingException("Failed to apply event", e);
        }
    }
}

// 4. CQRS Implementation
// Command Side
@Service
public class BlogCommandService {

    @Autowired
    private EventSourcingService eventSourcingService;

    @Autowired
    private BlogEventPublisher eventPublisher;

    public void createBlog(CreateBlogCommand command) {
        // Validate command
        validateCreateBlogCommand(command);

        // Create and save event
        BlogCreatedEvent event = BlogCreatedEvent.builder()
            .aggregateId(command.getBlogId())
            .title(command.getTitle())
            .content(command.getContent())
            .authorId(command.getAuthorId())
            .version(1L)
            .build();

        eventSourcingService.saveEvent(event);

        // Publish event for read model updates
        eventPublisher.publishBlogCreated(event);
    }

    public void updateBlog(UpdateBlogCommand command) {
        // Get current state
        BlogAggregate blog = eventSourcingService.rebuildAggregate(
            command.getBlogId(), BlogAggregate.class);

        // Validate command
        validateUpdateBlogCommand(command, blog);

        // Create and save event
        BlogUpdatedEvent event = BlogUpdatedEvent.builder()
            .aggregateId(command.getBlogId())
            .title(command.getTitle())
            .content(command.getContent())
            .version(blog.getVersion() + 1)
            .build();

        eventSourcingService.saveEvent(event);
        eventPublisher.publishBlogUpdated(event);
    }
}

// Query Side
@Service
public class BlogQueryService {

    @Autowired
    private BlogReadModelRepository readModelRepository;

    public BlogReadModel getBlog(String blogId) {
        return readModelRepository.findById(blogId)
            .orElseThrow(() -> new BlogNotFoundException("Blog not found: " + blogId));
    }

    public Page<BlogReadModel> getBlogs(Pageable pageable) {
        return readModelRepository.findByPublishedTrueOrderByCreatedAtDesc(pageable);
    }

    public List<BlogReadModel> searchBlogs(String query) {
        return readModelRepository.findByTitleContainingIgnoreCaseOrContentContainingIgnoreCase(
            query, query);
    }
}

// Read Model Updater
@Component
public class BlogReadModelUpdater {

    @Autowired
    private BlogReadModelRepository readModelRepository;

    @EventHandler
    public void handle(BlogCreatedEvent event) {
        BlogReadModel readModel = BlogReadModel.builder()
            .id(event.getAggregateId())
            .title(event.getTitle())
            .content(event.getContent())
            .authorId(event.getAuthorId())
            .published(false)
            .createdAt(LocalDateTime.now())
            .build();

        readModelRepository.save(readModel);
    }

    @EventHandler
    public void handle(BlogUpdatedEvent event) {
        readModelRepository.findById(event.getAggregateId())
            .ifPresent(readModel -> {
                readModel.setTitle(event.getTitle());
                readModel.setContent(event.getContent());
                readModel.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now());
                readModelRepository.save(readModel);
            });
    }

    @EventHandler
    public void handle(BlogDeletedEvent event) {
        readModelRepository.deleteById(event.getAggregateId());
    }
}
```

### **Tuần 32: Team Collaboration & Professional Soft Skills**

**Ngày 218-220: Git Workflow & Code Review Mastery**
```bash
# 1. ADVANCED GIT WORKFLOW - GitFlow Implementation

# Initialize GitFlow
git flow init

# Feature Development Workflow
git flow feature start new-blog-editor
# Work on feature...
git add .
git commit -m "feat: implement rich text editor for blog posts

- Add WYSIWYG editor with markdown support
- Implement image upload functionality
- Add auto-save feature
- Include spell check integration

Closes #123"

# Finish feature (merges to develop)
git flow feature finish new-blog-editor

# Release Workflow
git flow release start v1.2.0
# Update version numbers, changelog...
git flow release finish v1.2.0

# Hotfix Workflow
git flow hotfix start critical-security-fix
# Fix critical issue...
git flow hotfix finish critical-security-fix

# 2. CONVENTIONAL COMMITS Standard
# Format: <type>[optional scope]: <description>
#
# Types:
# feat: new feature
# fix: bug fix
# docs: documentation changes
# style: formatting, missing semicolons, etc.
# refactor: code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
# perf: performance improvement
# test: adding missing tests
# chore: maintain

# Examples:
git commit -m "feat(auth): add OAuth2 Google integration

- Implement Google OAuth2 authentication flow
- Add user profile synchronization
- Include proper error handling for auth failures
- Update security configuration

Breaking Change: Authentication endpoints now require OAuth2 tokens
Closes #456"

git commit -m "fix(blog): resolve XSS vulnerability in comment system

- Sanitize user input in comment content
- Add CSP headers for additional protection
- Update input validation rules
- Add security tests

Security: Fixes potential XSS attack vector
Fixes #789"

# 3. PULL REQUEST TEMPLATE
# .github/pull_request_template.md
```

**Pull Request Template:**
```markdown
## 📋 Description
Brief description of changes and motivation.

## 🔗 Related Issues
- Closes #123
- Related to #456

## 🧪 Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Code refactoring

## ✅ Testing
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Performance testing (if applicable)

## 📸 Screenshots (if applicable)
Before/After screenshots for UI changes

## 🔍 Code Review Checklist
- [ ] Code follows project style guidelines
- [ ] Self-review completed
- [ ] Comments added for complex logic
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] No console.log or debug code left
- [ ] Security considerations addressed

## 🚀 Deployment Notes
Any special deployment considerations or migration steps.
```

**Ngày 221-224: Agile/Scrum Methodology & Communication Skills**

## **AGILE/SCRUM IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE**

### **1. Sprint Planning Process**
```markdown
## Sprint Planning Template

### Sprint Goal
Clear, concise statement of what the team aims to achieve.

### Sprint Backlog
| Story | Priority | Estimate | Assignee | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------|----------|----------|-------------------|
| As a user, I want to... | High | 8 | John | Given/When/Then... |

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Code reviewed and approved
- [ ] Unit tests written and passing
- [ ] Integration tests passing
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Security review completed
- [ ] Performance tested
- [ ] Deployed to staging
- [ ] Product Owner approval

### Sprint Capacity
- Team velocity: 40 story points
- Available capacity: 36 story points (accounting for holidays/meetings)
- Committed work: 35 story points
```

### **2. Daily Standup Structure**
```markdown
## Daily Standup Format (15 minutes max)

### Each team member answers:
1. **What did I accomplish yesterday?**
   - Specific tasks completed
   - Any blockers resolved

2. **What will I work on today?**
   - Specific tasks planned
   - Expected outcomes

3. **Are there any impediments in my way?**
   - Technical blockers
   - Dependencies on other teams
   - Resource needs

### Scrum Master Notes:
- Track impediments for resolution
- Note any scope changes
- Identify collaboration opportunities
```

### **3. Sprint Review & Retrospective**
```markdown
## Sprint Review Template

### Demo Agenda
1. Sprint goal review
2. Completed user stories demonstration
3. Metrics review (velocity, burndown)
4. Stakeholder feedback collection
5. Next sprint preview

## Sprint Retrospective Template

### What went well? (Keep doing)
- Effective practices to continue

### What didn't go well? (Stop doing)
- Issues that hindered progress

### What should we try? (Start doing)
- New practices to experiment with

### Action Items
| Action | Owner | Due Date | Success Criteria |
|--------|-------|----------|------------------|
| Improve code review process | Team | Next sprint | Review time < 24h |
```

### **4. User Story Writing Best Practices**
```markdown
## User Story Template

### Format:
As a [type of user], I want [goal] so that [benefit].

### Example:
As a blog reader, I want to filter posts by category so that I can quickly find content relevant to my interests.

### Acceptance Criteria (Given/When/Then):
- Given I am on the blog page
- When I select a category filter
- Then I should see only posts from that category
- And the URL should update to reflect the filter
- And the page should load within 2 seconds

### Definition of Ready:
- [ ] Story is small enough to complete in one sprint
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are clear and testable
- [ ] Dependencies identified
- [ ] Design mockups available (if UI changes)
- [ ] Technical approach discussed
- [ ] Effort estimated by team
```

### **5. Technical Documentation Standards**
```markdown
## Code Documentation Template

### Function/Method Documentation:
```java
/**
 * Processes blog post creation with validation and event publishing.
 *
 * This method handles the complete blog creation workflow including:
 * - Input validation and sanitization
 * - Author authorization verification
 * - Content processing and storage
 * - Search index updates
 * - Event publishing for downstream services
 *
 * @param createBlogRequest The blog creation request containing title, content, and metadata
 * @param authorId The ID of the user creating the blog post
 * @return BlogResponse containing the created blog details and metadata
 * @throws ValidationException if the request data is invalid
 * @throws AuthorizationException if the user lacks permission to create blogs
 * @throws ServiceException if blog creation fails due to system issues
 *
 * @since 1.2.0
 * @see BlogValidationService#validateBlogRequest(CreateBlogRequest)
 * @see BlogEventPublisher#publishBlogCreated(Blog)
 */
public BlogResponse createBlog(CreateBlogRequest createBlogRequest, Long authorId) {
    // Implementation...
}
```

### **6. Client Communication Framework**
```markdown
## Client Communication Templates

### Project Status Update Email:
Subject: [Project Name] - Weekly Status Update - Week of [Date]

Dear [Client Name],

## Progress Summary
This week we completed [X] of [Y] planned features, maintaining our target delivery date of [Date].

## Completed This Week
- ✅ User authentication system
- ✅ Blog post creation functionality
- ✅ Basic search implementation

## In Progress
- 🔄 Advanced search filters (80% complete)
- 🔄 Email notification system (60% complete)

## Planned for Next Week
- 📋 User profile management
- 📋 Comment system implementation
- 📋 Performance optimization

## Risks & Mitigation
- **Risk**: Third-party API integration delay
- **Impact**: Potential 2-day delay in notification features
- **Mitigation**: Implementing fallback email service

## Questions/Decisions Needed
1. Approval needed for proposed UI changes to the dashboard
2. Clarification on user role permissions for admin features

Best regards,
[Your Name]
```

### **7. Requirements Gathering Process**
```markdown
## Requirements Gathering Checklist

### Discovery Phase
- [ ] Stakeholder interviews completed
- [ ] Business objectives documented
- [ ] User personas defined
- [ ] User journey maps created
- [ ] Technical constraints identified
- [ ] Integration requirements documented

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] User stories written and prioritized
- [ ] Acceptance criteria defined
- [ ] Business rules documented
- [ ] Data requirements specified
- [ ] Reporting needs identified

### Non-Functional Requirements
- [ ] Performance requirements (response times, throughput)
- [ ] Security requirements (authentication, authorization, data protection)
- [ ] Scalability requirements (concurrent users, data volume)
- [ ] Availability requirements (uptime, disaster recovery)
- [ ] Usability requirements (accessibility, browser support)
- [ ] Compliance requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, etc.)

### Technical Requirements
- [ ] Technology stack approved
- [ ] Architecture decisions documented
- [ ] Third-party integrations identified
- [ ] Infrastructure requirements specified
- [ ] Development environment setup
```

### **8. Problem-Solving Framework**
```markdown
## Systematic Problem-Solving Approach

### 1. Problem Definition (5W1H Method)
- **What** is the problem?
- **When** does it occur?
- **Where** does it happen?
- **Who** is affected?
- **Why** is it a problem?
- **How** can we measure the impact?

### 2. Root Cause Analysis
- Use "5 Whys" technique
- Create fishbone diagram for complex issues
- Gather data and evidence
- Involve relevant stakeholders

### 3. Solution Generation
- Brainstorm multiple solutions
- Consider short-term and long-term options
- Evaluate feasibility and impact
- Consider resource requirements

### 4. Decision Matrix
| Solution | Feasibility | Impact | Cost | Time | Risk | Total Score |
|----------|-------------|--------|------|------|------|-------------|
| Option A | 8 | 9 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 38 |
| Option B | 6 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 6 | 37 |

### 5. Implementation Plan
- Define clear action steps
- Assign responsibilities
- Set deadlines and milestones
- Identify success metrics
- Plan for monitoring and adjustment
```

### **9. Cost-Benefit Analysis Template**
```markdown
## Cost-Benefit Analysis Framework

### Project: [Feature/Initiative Name]

### Costs (One-time)
| Item | Cost | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Development time | $50,000 | 500 hours @ $100/hour |
| Infrastructure setup | $5,000 | Cloud services, tools |
| Training | $3,000 | Team training on new tech |
| **Total One-time** | **$58,000** | |

### Costs (Recurring Annual)
| Item | Annual Cost | Notes |
|------|-------------|-------|
| Maintenance | $12,000 | 20% of development cost |
| Infrastructure | $6,000 | Hosting, services |
| **Total Annual** | **$18,000** | |

### Benefits (Annual)
| Item | Annual Benefit | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| Time savings | $30,000 | 300 hours saved @ $100/hour |
| Increased revenue | $25,000 | 5% conversion improvement |
| Cost reduction | $10,000 | Automated processes |
| **Total Annual** | **$65,000** | |

### ROI Calculation
- **Year 1 Net Benefit**: $65,000 - $58,000 - $18,000 = -$11,000
- **Year 2 Net Benefit**: $65,000 - $18,000 = $47,000
- **Year 3 Net Benefit**: $65,000 - $18,000 = $47,000
- **3-Year ROI**: (($47,000 × 2 + (-$11,000)) / $58,000) × 100 = 143%
- **Payback Period**: 1.23 years
```

## **🚀 PHASE 12: ADVANCED DEVOPS & INFRASTRUCTURE (Tuần 33-34)**

### **Tuần 33: Complete Kubernetes Deployment**

**Ngày 225-227: Production-Ready Kubernetes Configuration**
```yaml
# k8s/namespace.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: cuonghoang-dev
  labels:
    name: cuonghoang-dev
    environment: production

---
# k8s/app-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: cuonghoang-app
  namespace: cuonghoang-dev
  labels:
    app: cuonghoang-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cuonghoang-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: cuonghoang-app
      annotations:
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        prometheus.io/port: "8080"
        prometheus.io/path: "/actuator/prometheus"
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cuonghoang-app
        image: cuonghoang/cuonghoang-dev:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        env:
        - name: SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE
          value: "kubernetes"
        - name: DB_USERNAME
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: app-secrets
              key: DB_USERNAME
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: app-secrets
              key: DB_PASSWORD
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /actuator/health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 30
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /actuator/health/readiness
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10

---
# k8s/ingress.yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: cuonghoang-ingress
  namespace: cuonghoang-dev
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rate-limit: "100"
spec:
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - cuonghoang.dev
    secretName: cuonghoang-tls
  rules:
  - host: cuonghoang.dev
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: cuonghoang-app-service
            port:
              number: 80

---
# k8s/hpa.yaml
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: cuonghoang-hpa
  namespace: cuonghoang-dev
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: cuonghoang-app
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
```

**Ngày 228-231: Infrastructure as Code với Terraform**
```hcl
# terraform/main.tf
terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.0"
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
    kubernetes = {
      source  = "hashicorp/kubernetes"
      version = "~> 2.0"
    }
  }

  backend "s3" {
    bucket = "cuonghoang-terraform-state"
    key    = "infrastructure/terraform.tfstate"
    region = "us-west-2"
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region

  default_tags {
    tags = {
      Project     = "CuongHoang-Dev"
      Environment = var.environment
      ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
    }
  }
}

# terraform/variables.tf
variable "aws_region" {
  description = "AWS region"
  type        = string
  default     = "us-west-2"
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment name"
  type        = string
  default     = "production"
}

variable "cluster_name" {
  description = "EKS cluster name"
  type        = string
  default     = "cuonghoang-eks"
}

variable "node_instance_type" {
  description = "EC2 instance type for EKS nodes"
  type        = string
  default     = "t3.medium"
}

variable "desired_capacity" {
  description = "Desired number of nodes"
  type        = number
  default     = 3
}

variable "max_capacity" {
  description = "Maximum number of nodes"
  type        = number
  default     = 10
}

variable "min_capacity" {
  description = "Minimum number of nodes"
  type        = number
  default     = 1
}

# terraform/vpc.tf
module "vpc" {
  source = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"

  name = "${var.cluster_name}-vpc"
  cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"

  azs             = ["${var.aws_region}a", "${var.aws_region}b", "${var.aws_region}c"]
  private_subnets = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24", "10.0.3.0/24"]
  public_subnets  = ["10.0.101.0/24", "10.0.102.0/24", "10.0.103.0/24"]

  enable_nat_gateway = true
  enable_vpn_gateway = false
  enable_dns_hostnames = true
  enable_dns_support = true

  public_subnet_tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/cluster/${var.cluster_name}" = "shared"
    "kubernetes.io/role/elb"                    = "1"
  }

  private_subnet_tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/cluster/${var.cluster_name}" = "shared"
    "kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb"           = "1"
  }
}

# terraform/eks.tf
module "eks" {
  source = "terraform-aws-modules/eks/aws"

  cluster_name    = var.cluster_name
  cluster_version = "1.28"

  vpc_id     = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets

  cluster_endpoint_private_access = true
  cluster_endpoint_public_access  = true

  cluster_addons = {
    coredns = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    kube-proxy = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    vpc-cni = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    aws-ebs-csi-driver = {
      most_recent = true
    }
  }

  eks_managed_node_groups = {
    main = {
      name = "${var.cluster_name}-nodes"

      instance_types = [var.node_instance_type]

      min_size     = var.min_capacity
      max_size     = var.max_capacity
      desired_size = var.desired_capacity

      disk_size = 50

      labels = {
        Environment = var.environment
        NodeGroup   = "main"
      }

      update_config = {
        max_unavailable_percentage = 25
      }
    }
  }

  cluster_security_group_additional_rules = {
    egress_nodes_ephemeral_ports_tcp = {
      description                = "To node 1025-65535"
      protocol                   = "tcp"
      from_port                  = 1025
      to_port                    = 65535
      type                       = "egress"
      source_node_security_group = true
    }
  }

  node_security_group_additional_rules = {
    ingress_self_all = {
      description = "Node to node all ports/protocols"
      protocol    = "-1"
      from_port   = 0
      to_port     = 0
      type        = "ingress"
      self        = true
    }
    egress_all = {
      description      = "Node all egress"
      protocol         = "-1"
      from_port        = 0
      to_port          = 0
      type             = "egress"
      cidr_blocks      = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
      ipv6_cidr_blocks = ["::/0"]
    }
  }
}

# terraform/rds.tf
resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "main" {
  name       = "${var.cluster_name}-db-subnet-group"
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets

  tags = {
    Name = "${var.cluster_name} DB subnet group"
  }
}

resource "aws_security_group" "rds" {
  name_prefix = "${var.cluster_name}-rds-"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id

  ingress {
    from_port   = 3306
    to_port     = 3306
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [module.vpc.vpc_cidr_block]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "${var.cluster_name}-rds-sg"
  }
}

resource "aws_db_instance" "main" {
  identifier = "${var.cluster_name}-mysql"

  engine         = "mysql"
  engine_version = "8.0"
  instance_class = "db.t3.micro"

  allocated_storage     = 20
  max_allocated_storage = 100
  storage_type          = "gp2"
  storage_encrypted     = true

  db_name  = "cuonghoang_dev"
  username = "cuonghoang"
  password = random_password.db_password.result

  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.rds.id]
  db_subnet_group_name   = aws_db_subnet_group.main.name

  backup_retention_period = 7
  backup_window          = "03:00-04:00"
  maintenance_window     = "sun:04:00-sun:05:00"

  skip_final_snapshot = true
  deletion_protection = false

  tags = {
    Name = "${var.cluster_name}-mysql"
  }
}

resource "random_password" "db_password" {
  length  = 16
  special = true
}

# terraform/elasticache.tf
resource "aws_elasticache_subnet_group" "main" {
  name       = "${var.cluster_name}-cache-subnet"
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets
}

resource "aws_security_group" "elasticache" {
  name_prefix = "${var.cluster_name}-elasticache-"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id

  ingress {
    from_port   = 6379
    to_port     = 6379
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [module.vpc.vpc_cidr_block]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "${var.cluster_name}-elasticache-sg"
  }
}

resource "aws_elasticache_replication_group" "main" {
  replication_group_id       = "${var.cluster_name}-redis"
  description                = "Redis cluster for ${var.cluster_name}"

  node_type                  = "cache.t3.micro"
  port                       = 6379
  parameter_group_name       = "default.redis7"

  num_cache_clusters         = 2
  automatic_failover_enabled = true
  multi_az_enabled          = true

  subnet_group_name = aws_elasticache_subnet_group.main.name
  security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.elasticache.id]

  at_rest_encryption_enabled = true
  transit_encryption_enabled = true

  tags = {
    Name = "${var.cluster_name}-redis"
  }
}

# terraform/outputs.tf
output "cluster_endpoint" {
  description = "Endpoint for EKS control plane"
  value       = module.eks.cluster_endpoint
}

output "cluster_security_group_id" {
  description = "Security group ids attached to the cluster control plane"
  value       = module.eks.cluster_security_group_id
}

output "cluster_iam_role_name" {
  description = "IAM role name associated with EKS cluster"
  value       = module.eks.cluster_iam_role_name
}

output "cluster_certificate_authority_data" {
  description = "Base64 encoded certificate data required to communicate with the cluster"
  value       = module.eks.cluster_certificate_authority_data
}

output "cluster_name" {
  description = "The name/id of the EKS cluster"
  value       = module.eks.cluster_name
}

output "rds_endpoint" {
  description = "RDS instance endpoint"
  value       = aws_db_instance.main.endpoint
  sensitive   = true
}

output "redis_endpoint" {
  description = "Redis cluster endpoint"
  value       = aws_elasticache_replication_group.main.primary_endpoint_address
  sensitive   = true
}
```

### **Tuần 34: Complete ELK Stack & Monitoring**

**Ngày 232-235: Complete ELK Stack Implementation**
```yaml
# elk/elasticsearch.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  serviceName: elasticsearch
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
        - containerPort: 9300
        env:
        - name: cluster.name
          value: "cuonghoang-cluster"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: discovery.seed_hosts
          value: "elasticsearch-0.elasticsearch,elasticsearch-1.elasticsearch,elasticsearch-2.elasticsearch"
        - name: cluster.initial_master_nodes
          value: "elasticsearch-0,elasticsearch-1,elasticsearch-2"
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        - name: xpack.security.enabled
          value: "false"
        - name: xpack.monitoring.collection.enabled
          value: "true"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: elasticsearch-data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: elasticsearch-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi

---
# elk/logstash.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: logstash-config
  namespace: monitoring
data:
  logstash.yml: |
    http.host: "0.0.0.0"
    path.config: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
    xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]

  logstash.conf: |
    input {
      beats {
        port => 5044
      }
      tcp {
        port => 5000
        codec => json_lines
      }
    }

    filter {
      if [fields][service] == "cuonghoang-app" {
        grok {
          match => {
            "message" => "%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:timestamp} %{LOGLEVEL:level} %{DATA:thread} %{DATA:logger} - %{GREEDYDATA:log_message}"
          }
        }

        date {
          match => [ "timestamp", "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS" ]
        }

        if [level] == "ERROR" {
          mutate {
            add_tag => ["error"]
          }
        }

        if [log_message] =~ /Exception/ {
          mutate {
            add_tag => ["exception"]
          }
        }

        # Parse JSON logs
        if [log_message] =~ /^\{.*\}$/ {
          json {
            source => "log_message"
            target => "parsed_json"
          }
        }

        # Extract performance metrics
        if [log_message] =~ /Request processed in/ {
          grok {
            match => {
              "log_message" => "Request processed in %{NUMBER:response_time:float}ms"
            }
          }
        }

        # Extract user information
        if [log_message] =~ /User:/ {
          grok {
            match => {
              "log_message" => "User: %{DATA:user_id}"
            }
          }
        }
      }

      # Parse Nginx access logs
      if [fields][service] == "nginx" {
        grok {
          match => {
            "message" => "%{NGINXACCESS}"
          }
        }

        mutate {
          convert => {
            "response" => "integer"
            "bytes" => "integer"
            "responsetime" => "float"
          }
        }

        date {
          match => [ "timestamp", "dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss Z" ]
        }

        if [response] >= 400 {
          mutate {
            add_tag => ["error"]
          }
        }

        if [response] >= 500 {
          mutate {
            add_tag => ["server_error"]
          }
        }
      }
    }

    output {
      elasticsearch {
        hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
        index => "cuonghoang-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
        template_name => "cuonghoang-template"
        template => "/usr/share/logstash/templates/cuonghoang-template.json"
        template_overwrite => true
      }

      # Output errors to separate index
      if "error" in [tags] {
        elasticsearch {
          hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
          index => "cuonghoang-errors-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
        }
      }

      # Debug output
      stdout {
        codec => rubydebug
      }
    }

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: logstash
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: logstash
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: logstash
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: logstash
        image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.11.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5044
        - containerPort: 5000
        - containerPort: 9600
        env:
        - name: LS_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xmx512m -Xms512m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: logstash-config
          mountPath: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
        - name: logstash-settings
          mountPath: /usr/share/logstash/config
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
      volumes:
      - name: logstash-config
        configMap:
          name: logstash-config
          items:
          - key: logstash.conf
            path: logstash.conf
      - name: logstash-settings
        configMap:
          name: logstash-config
          items:
          - key: logstash.yml
            path: logstash.yml

---
# elk/kibana.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: kibana-config
  namespace: monitoring
data:
  kibana.yml: |
    server.host: "0.0.0.0"
    server.port: 5601
    elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
    monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled: true
    xpack.security.enabled: false
    xpack.monitoring.enabled: true

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kibana
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: kibana
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: kibana
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: kibana
        image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.11.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5601
        env:
        - name: ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS
          value: "http://elasticsearch:9200"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: kibana-config
          mountPath: /usr/share/kibana/config
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /api/status
            port: 5601
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 10
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /api/status
            port: 5601
          initialDelaySeconds: 120
          periodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - name: kibana-config
        configMap:
          name: kibana-config

---
# elk/filebeat.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-config
  namespace: monitoring
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
    - type: container
      paths:
        - /var/log/containers/*.log
      processors:
        - add_kubernetes_metadata:
            host: ${NODE_NAME}
            matchers:
            - logs_path:
                logs_path: "/var/log/containers/"

    - type: log
      paths:
        - /var/log/nginx/access.log
        - /var/log/nginx/error.log
      fields:
        service: nginx
      fields_under_root: true

    output.logstash:
      hosts: ["logstash:5044"]

    processors:
      - add_host_metadata:
          when.not.contains.tags: forwarded
      - add_cloud_metadata: ~
      - add_docker_metadata: ~
      - add_kubernetes_metadata: ~

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: filebeat
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: filebeat
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: filebeat
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: filebeat
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      hostNetwork: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirstWithHostNet
      containers:
      - name: filebeat
        image: docker.elastic.co/beats/filebeat:8.11.0
        args: [
          "-c", "/etc/filebeat.yml",
          "-e",
        ]
        env:
        - name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: spec.nodeName
        securityContext:
          runAsUser: 0
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 200Mi
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 100Mi
        volumeMounts:
        - name: config
          mountPath: /etc/filebeat.yml
          readOnly: true
          subPath: filebeat.yml
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/filebeat/data
        - name: varlibdockercontainers
          mountPath: /var/lib/docker/containers
          readOnly: true
        - name: varlog
          mountPath: /var/log
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          defaultMode: 0640
          name: filebeat-config
      - name: varlibdockercontainers
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/docker/containers
      - name: varlog
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log
      - name: data
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/filebeat-data
          type: DirectoryOrCreate

---
# elk/services.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
  - name: rest
    port: 9200
  - name: inter-node
    port: 9300

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: logstash
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    app: logstash
  ports:
  - name: beats
    port: 5044
  - name: tcp
    port: 5000
  - name: http
    port: 9600

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: kibana
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    app: kibana
  ports:
  - port: 5601
    targetPort: 5601
  type: LoadBalancer
```

**Ngày 236-238: Complete Prometheus & Grafana Setup**
```yaml
# monitoring/prometheus.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-config
  namespace: monitoring
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
      evaluation_interval: 15s

    rule_files:
      - "/etc/prometheus/rules/*.yml"

    alerting:
      alertmanagers:
        - static_configs:
            - targets:
              - alertmanager:9093

    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: 'prometheus'
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['localhost:9090']

      - job_name: 'kubernetes-apiservers'
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
        - role: endpoints
        scheme: https
        tls_config:
          ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
        bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
        relabel_configs:
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace, __meta_kubernetes_service_name, __meta_kubernetes_endpoint_port_name]
          action: keep
          regex: default;kubernetes;https

      - job_name: 'kubernetes-nodes'
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
        - role: node
        scheme: https
        tls_config:
          ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
        bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
        relabel_configs:
        - action: labelmap
          regex: __meta_kubernetes_node_label_(.+)
        - target_label: __address__
          replacement: kubernetes.default.svc:443
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_node_name]
          regex: (.+)
          target_label: __metrics_path__
          replacement: /api/v1/nodes/${1}/proxy/metrics

      - job_name: 'kubernetes-pods'
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
        - role: pod
        relabel_configs:
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape]
          action: keep
          regex: true
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
          action: replace
          target_label: __metrics_path__
          regex: (.+)
        - source_labels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
          action: replace
          regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
          replacement: $1:$2
          target_label: __address__
        - action: labelmap
          regex: __meta_kubernetes_pod_label_(.+)
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
          action: replace
          target_label: kubernetes_namespace
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
          action: replace
          target_label: kubernetes_pod_name

      - job_name: 'cuonghoang-app'
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
        - role: pod
          namespaces:
            names:
            - cuonghoang-dev
        relabel_configs:
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_label_app]
          action: keep
          regex: cuonghoang-app
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape]
          action: keep
          regex: true
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
          action: replace
          target_label: __metrics_path__
          regex: (.+)
        - source_labels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
          action: replace
          regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
          replacement: $1:$2
          target_label: __address__
        - action: labelmap
          regex: __meta_kubernetes_pod_label_(.+)
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
          action: replace
          target_label: kubernetes_namespace
        - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
          action: replace
          target_label: kubernetes_pod_name

      - job_name: 'mysql-exporter'
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['mysql-exporter:9104']

      - job_name: 'redis-exporter'
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['redis-exporter:9121']

      - job_name: 'nginx-exporter'
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['nginx-exporter:9113']

  alert_rules.yml: |
    groups:
    - name: cuonghoang-app-alerts
      rules:
      - alert: HighErrorRate
        expr: rate(http_requests_total{status=~"5.."}[5m]) > 0.1
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "High error rate detected"
          description: "Error rate is {{ $value }} errors per second"

      - alert: HighResponseTime
        expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 1
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "High response time detected"
          description: "95th percentile response time is {{ $value }} seconds"

      - alert: HighMemoryUsage
        expr: (container_memory_usage_bytes / container_spec_memory_limit_bytes) > 0.8
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "High memory usage detected"
          description: "Memory usage is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}"

      - alert: HighCPUUsage
        expr: rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total[5m]) > 0.8
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "High CPU usage detected"
          description: "CPU usage is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}"

      - alert: PodCrashLooping
        expr: rate(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[15m]) > 0
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Pod is crash looping"
          description: "Pod {{ $labels.pod }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} is crash looping"

      - alert: DatabaseDown
        expr: mysql_up == 0
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "MySQL database is down"
          description: "MySQL database has been down for more than 1 minute"

      - alert: RedisDown
        expr: redis_up == 0
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Redis is down"
          description: "Redis has been down for more than 1 minute"

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: prometheus
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: prometheus
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: prometheus
      containers:
      - name: prometheus
        image: prom/prometheus:v2.47.0
        args:
          - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
          - '--storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus/'
          - '--web.console.libraries=/etc/prometheus/console_libraries'
          - '--web.console.templates=/etc/prometheus/consoles'
          - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d'
          - '--web.enable-lifecycle'
          - '--web.enable-admin-api'
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9090
        volumeMounts:
        - name: prometheus-config
          mountPath: /etc/prometheus/
        - name: prometheus-storage
          mountPath: /prometheus/
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
      volumes:
      - name: prometheus-config
        configMap:
          name: prometheus-config
      - name: prometheus-storage
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: prometheus-pvc

---
# monitoring/grafana.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: grafana-datasources
  namespace: monitoring
data:
  prometheus.yaml: |
    apiVersion: 1
    datasources:
    - name: Prometheus
      type: prometheus
      url: http://prometheus:9090
      access: proxy
      isDefault: true
      editable: true

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grafana
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: grafana
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: grafana
        image: grafana/grafana:10.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000
        env:
        - name: GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD
          value: "admin123"
        - name: GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS
          value: "grafana-kubernetes-app"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: grafana-storage
          mountPath: /var/lib/grafana
        - name: grafana-datasources
          mountPath: /etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "125m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
      volumes:
      - name: grafana-storage
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: grafana-pvc
      - name: grafana-datasources
        configMap:
          name: grafana-datasources
```
