```java
// Enhanced OpenAPI Configuration
@Configuration
@OpenAPIDefinition(
    info = @Info(
        title = "CuongHoang Dev Portfolio API",
        version = "v1.0.0",
        description = "Complete REST API for CuongHoang Developer Portfolio Platform",
        contact = @Contact(
            name = "CuongHoang",
            email = "contact@cuonghoang.dev",
            url = "https://cuonghoang.dev"
        ),
        license = @License(
            name = "MIT License",
            url = "https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT"
        )
    ),
    servers = {
        @Server(url = "https://api.cuonghoang.dev", description = "Production Server"),
        @Server(url = "https://staging-api.cuonghoang.dev", description = "Staging Server"),
        @Server(url = "http://localhost:8080", description = "Development Server")
    },
    security = {
        @SecurityRequirement(name = "bearerAuth"),
        @SecurityRequirement(name = "apiKey")
    }
)
@SecuritySchemes({
    @SecurityScheme(
        name = "bearerAuth",
        type = SecuritySchemeType.HTTP,
        scheme = "bearer",
        bearerFormat = "JWT",
        description = "JWT Bearer Token Authentication"
    ),
    @SecurityScheme(
        name = "apiKey",
        type = SecuritySchemeType.APIKEY,
        in = SecuritySchemeIn.HEADER,
        paramName = "X-API-Key",
        description = "API Key Authentication"
    )
})
public class OpenApiConfig {

    @Bean
    public OpenAPI customOpenAPI() {
        return new OpenAPI()
            .components(new Components()
                .addSchemas("Error", new Schema<>()
                    .type("object")
                    .addProperty("timestamp", new Schema<>().type("string").format("date-time"))
                    .addProperty("status", new Schema<>().type("integer"))
                    .addProperty("error", new Schema<>().type("string"))
                    .addProperty("message", new Schema<>().type("string"))
                    .addProperty("path", new Schema<>().type("string"))
                    .addProperty("errors", new Schema<>().type("array")
                        .items(new Schema<>()
                            .type("object")
                            .addProperty("field", new Schema<>().type("string"))
                            .addProperty("message", new Schema<>().type("string"))
                            .addProperty("rejectedValue", new Schema<>().type("object"))
                        )
                    )
                )
                .addSchemas("PageInfo", new Schema<>()
                    .type("object")
                    .addProperty("page", new Schema<>().type("integer").description("Current page number (0-based)"))
                    .addProperty("size", new Schema<>().type("integer").description("Number of items per page"))
                    .addProperty("totalElements", new Schema<>().type("integer").description("Total number of items"))
                    .addProperty("totalPages", new Schema<>().type("integer").description("Total number of pages"))
                    .addProperty("first", new Schema<>().type("boolean").description("Whether this is the first page"))
                    .addProperty("last", new Schema<>().type("boolean").description("Whether this is the last page"))
                )
            );
    }
}

// Enhanced Blog Controller with Complete Documentation
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api/blogs")
@Tag(name = "Blog Management", description = "Operations for managing blog posts")
@Validated
public class BlogController {

    @Autowired
    private BlogService blogService;

    @Operation(
        summary = "Get all published blogs",
        description = "Retrieve a paginated list of all published blog posts with optional filtering and sorting",
        responses = {
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "200",
                description = "Successfully retrieved blogs",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(implementation = PagedBlogResponse.class),
                    examples = @ExampleObject(
                        name = "Successful Response",
                        value = """
                        {
                          "content": [
                            {
                              "id": 1,
                              "title": "Getting Started with Spring Boot",
                              "excerpt": "Learn the basics of Spring Boot development...",
                              "slug": "getting-started-spring-boot",
                              "featuredImage": "https://cdn.cuonghoang.dev/images/spring-boot.jpg",
                              "publishedAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
                              "author": {
                                "id": 1,
                                "name": "CuongHoang",
                                "avatar": "https://cdn.cuonghoang.dev/avatars/cuonghoang.jpg"
                              },
                              "category": {
                                "id": 1,
                                "name": "Technology",
                                "slug": "technology"
                              },
                              "tags": ["Spring Boot", "Java", "Backend"],
                              "readingTime": 5,
                              "viewCount": 1250
                            }
                          ],
                          "pageable": {
                            "page": 0,
                            "size": 10,
                            "totalElements": 25,
                            "totalPages": 3,
                            "first": true,
                            "last": false
                          }
                        }
                        """
                    )
                )
            ),
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "400",
                description = "Invalid request parameters",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(ref = "#/components/schemas/Error")
                )
            )
        }
    )
    @GetMapping
    public ResponseEntity<Page<BlogResponse>> getAllBlogs(
        @Parameter(description = "Page number (0-based)", example = "0")
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "0") @Min(0) int page,

        @Parameter(description = "Number of items per page", example = "10")
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "10") @Min(1) @Max(100) int size,

        @Parameter(description = "Sort field", example = "publishedAt")
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "publishedAt") String sortBy,

        @Parameter(description = "Sort direction", example = "desc")
        @RequestParam(defaultValue = "desc") String sortDir,

        @Parameter(description = "Filter by category slug", example = "technology")
        @RequestParam(required = false) String category,

        @Parameter(description = "Filter by tag", example = "spring-boot")
        @RequestParam(required = false) String tag,

        @Parameter(description = "Search query", example = "spring boot tutorial")
        @RequestParam(required = false) String search
    ) {
        BlogSearchCriteria criteria = BlogSearchCriteria.builder()
            .category(category)
            .tag(tag)
            .search(search)
            .build();

        Pageable pageable = PageRequest.of(page, size,
            Sort.by(Sort.Direction.fromString(sortDir), sortBy));

        Page<BlogResponse> blogs = blogService.getPublishedBlogs(criteria, pageable);
        return ResponseEntity.ok(blogs);
    }

    @Operation(
        summary = "Get blog by slug",
        description = "Retrieve a specific blog post by its slug identifier",
        responses = {
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "200",
                description = "Blog found successfully",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(implementation = BlogDetailResponse.class)
                )
            ),
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "404",
                description = "Blog not found",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(ref = "#/components/schemas/Error")
                )
            )
        }
    )
    @GetMapping("/{slug}")
    public ResponseEntity<BlogDetailResponse> getBlogBySlug(
        @Parameter(description = "Blog slug identifier", example = "getting-started-spring-boot")
        @PathVariable @NotBlank String slug
    ) {
        BlogDetailResponse blog = blogService.getBlogBySlug(slug);
        return ResponseEntity.ok(blog);
    }

    @Operation(
        summary = "Create new blog post",
        description = "Create a new blog post (requires authentication)",
        security = @SecurityRequirement(name = "bearerAuth"),
        responses = {
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "201",
                description = "Blog created successfully",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(implementation = BlogResponse.class)
                )
            ),
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "400",
                description = "Invalid blog data",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(ref = "#/components/schemas/Error")
                )
            ),
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "401",
                description = "Authentication required",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(ref = "#/components/schemas/Error")
                )
            ),
            @ApiResponse(
                responseCode = "403",
                description = "Insufficient permissions",
                content = @Content(
                    mediaType = "application/json",
                    schema = @Schema(ref = "#/components/schemas/Error")
                )
            )
        }
    )
    @PostMapping
    @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') or hasRole('AUTHOR')")
    public ResponseEntity<BlogResponse> createBlog(
        @Parameter(description = "Blog creation data")
        @Valid @RequestBody CreateBlogRequest request,
        Authentication authentication
    ) {
        BlogResponse blog = blogService.createBlog(request, authentication.getName());
        return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.CREATED).body(blog);
    }
}

// Complete DTOs with Documentation
@Schema(description = "Blog creation request")
public class CreateBlogRequest {

    @Schema(description = "Blog title", example = "Getting Started with Spring Boot", required = true)
    @NotBlank(message = "Title is required")
    @Size(min = 5, max = 200, message = "Title must be between 5 and 200 characters")
    private String title;

    @Schema(description = "Blog content in Markdown format", required = true)
    @NotBlank(message = "Content is required")
    @Size(min = 100, message = "Content must be at least 100 characters")
    private String content;

    @Schema(description = "Brief excerpt or summary", example = "Learn the basics of Spring Boot development...")
    @Size(max = 500, message = "Excerpt cannot exceed 500 characters")
    private String excerpt;

    @Schema(description = "Category ID", example = "1", required = true)
    @NotNull(message = "Category is required")
    @Positive(message = "Category ID must be positive")
    private Long categoryId;

    @Schema(description = "List of tags", example = "[\"Spring Boot\", \"Java\", \"Backend\"]")
    @Size(max = 10, message = "Maximum 10 tags allowed")
    private List<@NotBlank @Size(max = 50) String> tags = new ArrayList<>();

    @Schema(description = "Featured image URL", example = "https://cdn.cuonghoang.dev/images/spring-boot.jpg")
    @URL(message = "Featured image must be a valid URL")
    private String featuredImage;

    @Schema(description = "Whether to publish immediately", example = "false")
    private boolean published = false;

    @Schema(description = "Scheduled publish date (ISO 8601 format)", example = "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z")
    @Future(message = "Scheduled date must be in the future")
    private LocalDateTime scheduledAt;

    // Getters and setters...
}

@Schema(description = "Blog response with basic information")
public class BlogResponse {

    @Schema(description = "Unique blog identifier", example = "1")
    private Long id;

    @Schema(description = "Blog title", example = "Getting Started with Spring Boot")
    private String title;

    @Schema(description = "Brief excerpt", example = "Learn the basics of Spring Boot development...")
    private String excerpt;

    @Schema(description = "URL-friendly slug", example = "getting-started-spring-boot")
    private String slug;

    @Schema(description = "Featured image URL", example = "https://cdn.cuonghoang.dev/images/spring-boot.jpg")
    private String featuredImage;

    @Schema(description = "Publication date", example = "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z")
    private LocalDateTime publishedAt;

    @Schema(description = "Blog author information")
    private AuthorResponse author;

    @Schema(description = "Blog category")
    private CategoryResponse category;

    @Schema(description = "List of tags", example = "[\"Spring Boot\", \"Java\", \"Backend\"]")
    private List<String> tags;

    @Schema(description = "Estimated reading time in minutes", example = "5")
    private Integer readingTime;

    @Schema(description = "Number of views", example = "1250")
    private Long viewCount;

    // Getters and setters...
}

// Performance Tuning Configuration
@Configuration
@EnableConfigurationProperties(PerformanceProperties.class)
public class PerformanceConfig {

    @Bean
    @ConditionalOnProperty(name = "app.performance.async.enabled", havingValue = "true")
    public TaskExecutor taskExecutor() {
        ThreadPoolTaskExecutor executor = new ThreadPoolTaskExecutor();
        executor.setCorePoolSize(10);
        executor.setMaxPoolSize(50);
        executor.setQueueCapacity(100);
        executor.setThreadNamePrefix("async-");
        executor.setRejectedExecutionHandler(new ThreadPoolExecutor.CallerRunsPolicy());
        executor.setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true);
        executor.setAwaitTerminationSeconds(60);
        executor.initialize();
        return executor;
    }

    @Bean
    public CacheManager cacheManager() {
        CaffeineCacheManager cacheManager = new CaffeineCacheManager();
        cacheManager.setCaffeine(Caffeine.newBuilder()
            .maximumSize(1000)
            .expireAfterWrite(Duration.ofMinutes(10))
            .recordStats());
        return cacheManager;
    }

    @Bean
    @ConditionalOnProperty(name = "app.performance.compression.enabled", havingValue = "true")
    public FilterRegistrationBean<CompressionFilter> compressionFilter() {
        FilterRegistrationBean<CompressionFilter> registration = new FilterRegistrationBean<>();
        registration.setFilter(new CompressionFilter());
        registration.addUrlPatterns("/api/*");
        registration.setOrder(1);
        return registration;
    }
}

@ConfigurationProperties(prefix = "app.performance")
@Data
public class PerformanceProperties {

    private Async async = new Async();
    private Cache cache = new Cache();
    private Compression compression = new Compression();
    private Database database = new Database();

    @Data
    public static class Async {
        private boolean enabled = true;
        private int corePoolSize = 10;
        private int maxPoolSize = 50;
        private int queueCapacity = 100;
    }

    @Data
    public static class Cache {
        private boolean enabled = true;
        private long maximumSize = 1000;
        private Duration expireAfterWrite = Duration.ofMinutes(10);
        private Duration expireAfterAccess = Duration.ofMinutes(5);
    }

    @Data
    public static class Compression {
        private boolean enabled = true;
        private int minResponseSize = 1024;
        private List<String> mimeTypes = Arrays.asList(
            "application/json",
            "application/xml",
            "text/html",
            "text/css",
            "text/javascript"
        );
    }

    @Data
    public static class Database {
        private boolean optimizationEnabled = true;
        private int batchSize = 50;
        private boolean showSql = false;
        private boolean formatSql = false;
        private boolean useSecondLevelCache = true;
        private boolean useQueryCache = true;
    }
}
```

### **Tuần 36: Complete Performance Tuning & Deployment Guides**

**Ngày 243-245: Comprehensive Performance Tuning Guide**

## **🚀 COMPLETE PERFORMANCE TUNING GUIDE**

### **1. JVM Performance Tuning**
```bash
# JVM Heap Tuning
-Xms2g                    # Initial heap size
-Xmx4g                    # Maximum heap size
-XX:NewRatio=3            # Old/Young generation ratio
-XX:SurvivorRatio=8       # Eden/Survivor space ratio

# Garbage Collection Tuning
-XX:+UseG1GC              # Use G1 garbage collector
-XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=200  # Target pause time
-XX:G1HeapRegionSize=16m  # G1 region size
-XX:+UseStringDeduplication # String deduplication

# Memory Management
-XX:MetaspaceSize=256m    # Initial metaspace size
-XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m # Maximum metaspace size
-XX:CompressedClassSpaceSize=128m

# Performance Monitoring
-XX:+PrintGC              # Print GC details
-XX:+PrintGCDetails       # Detailed GC information
-XX:+PrintGCTimeStamps    # GC timestamps
-Xloggc:/var/log/gc.log   # GC log file

# JIT Compiler Tuning
-XX:+TieredCompilation    # Enable tiered compilation
-XX:TieredStopAtLevel=4   # Use C2 compiler
-XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=256m # Code cache size

# Example production JVM settings
JAVA_OPTS="-server -Xms2g -Xmx4g -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=200 \
           -XX:+UseStringDeduplication -XX:MetaspaceSize=256m \
           -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m -XX:+PrintGCDetails \
           -XX:+PrintGCTimeStamps -Xloggc:/var/log/gc.log \
           -XX:+UseGCLogFileRotation -XX:NumberOfGCLogFiles=5 \
           -XX:GCLogFileSize=100M"
```

### **2. Database Performance Optimization**
```sql
-- MySQL Performance Tuning

-- 1. Query Optimization
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM blogs WHERE category_id = 1 AND published = true;

-- Add proper indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_blogs_category_published ON blogs(category_id, published);
CREATE INDEX idx_blogs_created_at ON blogs(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_blogs_slug ON blogs(slug);

-- 2. Connection Pool Optimization
-- application.yml
spring:
  datasource:
    hikari:
      maximum-pool-size: 20
      minimum-idle: 5
      connection-timeout: 30000
      idle-timeout: 600000
      max-lifetime: 1800000
      leak-detection-threshold: 60000

-- 3. MySQL Configuration (my.cnf)
[mysqld]
# Buffer Pool Settings
innodb_buffer_pool_size = 2G
innodb_buffer_pool_instances = 8

# Log Settings
innodb_log_file_size = 256M
innodb_log_buffer_size = 16M
innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit = 2

# Query Cache
query_cache_type = 1
query_cache_size = 256M
query_cache_limit = 2M

# Connection Settings
max_connections = 200
max_connect_errors = 100000
wait_timeout = 28800
interactive_timeout = 28800

# Performance Schema
performance_schema = ON
performance_schema_max_table_instances = 12500

-- 4. Slow Query Analysis
SET GLOBAL slow_query_log = 'ON';
SET GLOBAL long_query_time = 2;
SET GLOBAL log_queries_not_using_indexes = 'ON';

-- Analyze slow queries
SELECT
    query_time,
    lock_time,
    rows_sent,
    rows_examined,
    sql_text
FROM mysql.slow_log
ORDER BY query_time DESC
LIMIT 10;
```

### **3. Application-Level Performance Optimization**
```java
// 1. Caching Strategy Implementation
@Service
public class PerformanceOptimizedBlogService {

    @Cacheable(value = "blogs", key = "#slug")
    public BlogResponse getBlogBySlug(String slug) {
        return blogRepository.findBySlug(slug)
            .map(this::mapToBlogResponse)
            .orElseThrow(() -> new BlogNotFoundException("Blog not found: " + slug));
    }

    @CacheEvict(value = "blogs", key = "#blog.slug")
    public BlogResponse updateBlog(Blog blog) {
        Blog savedBlog = blogRepository.save(blog);
        return mapToBlogResponse(savedBlog);
    }

    // Batch processing for better performance
    @Transactional
    public List<BlogResponse> createBlogsInBatch(List<CreateBlogRequest> requests) {
        List<Blog> blogs = requests.stream()
            .map(this::mapToBlog)
            .collect(Collectors.toList());

        // Use batch insert
        List<Blog> savedBlogs = blogRepository.saveAll(blogs);

        return savedBlogs.stream()
            .map(this::mapToBlogResponse)
            .collect(Collectors.toList());
    }
}

// 2. Async Processing for Heavy Operations
@Service
public class AsyncProcessingService {

    @Async("taskExecutor")
    public CompletableFuture<Void> processImageUpload(String imageUrl) {
        try {
            // Image processing logic
            imageProcessingService.optimizeImage(imageUrl);
            imageProcessingService.generateThumbnails(imageUrl);

            return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(null);
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(e);
        }
    }

    @Async("emailExecutor")
    public CompletableFuture<Void> sendNotificationEmail(String email, String subject, String content) {
        try {
            emailService.sendEmail(email, subject, content);
            return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(null);
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(e);
        }
    }
}

// 3. Connection Pool Monitoring
@Component
public class ConnectionPoolMonitor {

    @Autowired
    private HikariDataSource dataSource;

    @Scheduled(fixedRate = 30000) // Every 30 seconds
    public void monitorConnectionPool() {
        HikariPoolMXBean poolBean = dataSource.getHikariPoolMXBean();

        log.info("Connection Pool Stats - Active: {}, Idle: {}, Total: {}, Waiting: {}",
            poolBean.getActiveConnections(),
            poolBean.getIdleConnections(),
            poolBean.getTotalConnections(),
            poolBean.getThreadsAwaitingConnection());

        // Alert if pool is under stress
        if (poolBean.getActiveConnections() > poolBean.getTotalConnections() * 0.8) {
            log.warn("Connection pool is under stress! Consider increasing pool size.");
        }
    }
}
```

### **4. Memory Management & Monitoring**
```java
// Memory Usage Monitoring
@Component
public class MemoryMonitor {

    private final MeterRegistry meterRegistry;

    public MemoryMonitor(MeterRegistry meterRegistry) {
        this.meterRegistry = meterRegistry;
        setupMemoryMetrics();
    }

    private void setupMemoryMetrics() {
        // JVM Memory metrics
        Gauge.builder("jvm.memory.heap.used")
            .register(meterRegistry, this, obj -> getHeapMemoryUsed());

        Gauge.builder("jvm.memory.heap.max")
            .register(meterRegistry, this, obj -> getHeapMemoryMax());

        Gauge.builder("jvm.memory.non-heap.used")
            .register(meterRegistry, this, obj -> getNonHeapMemoryUsed());
    }

    private double getHeapMemoryUsed() {
        return ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean().getHeapMemoryUsage().getUsed();
    }

    private double getHeapMemoryMax() {
        return ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean().getHeapMemoryUsage().getMax();
    }

    private double getNonHeapMemoryUsed() {
        return ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean().getNonHeapMemoryUsage().getUsed();
    }

    @EventListener
    public void handleMemoryWarning(MemoryWarningEvent event) {
        log.warn("Memory warning detected: {}", event.getMessage());

        // Trigger garbage collection
        System.gc();

        // Clear caches if necessary
        cacheManager.getCacheNames().forEach(cacheName -> {
            Cache cache = cacheManager.getCache(cacheName);
            if (cache != null) {
                cache.clear();
            }
        });
    }
}

// Memory Leak Detection
@Component
public class MemoryLeakDetector {

    private final Map<String, Long> memoryBaseline = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();

    @Scheduled(fixedRate = 300000) // Every 5 minutes
    public void detectMemoryLeaks() {
        Runtime runtime = Runtime.getRuntime();
        long currentMemory = runtime.totalMemory() - runtime.freeMemory();

        String timestamp = LocalDateTime.now().toString();
        memoryBaseline.put(timestamp, currentMemory);

        // Keep only last 12 measurements (1 hour)
        if (memoryBaseline.size() > 12) {
            String oldestKey = memoryBaseline.keySet().iterator().next();
            memoryBaseline.remove(oldestKey);
        }

        // Check for memory leak pattern
        if (memoryBaseline.size() >= 6) {
            List<Long> recentMemory = memoryBaseline.values().stream()
                .skip(memoryBaseline.size() - 6)
                .collect(Collectors.toList());

            boolean isIncreasing = true;
            for (int i = 1; i < recentMemory.size(); i++) {
                if (recentMemory.get(i) <= recentMemory.get(i - 1)) {
                    isIncreasing = false;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (isIncreasing) {
                log.warn("Potential memory leak detected! Memory usage consistently increasing.");
                // Trigger heap dump for analysis
                generateHeapDump();
            }
        }
    }

    private void generateHeapDump() {
        try {
            MBeanServer server = ManagementFactory.getPlatformMBeanServer();
            HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean mxBean = ManagementFactory.newPlatformMXBeanProxy(
                server, "com.sun.management:type=HotSpotDiagnostic", HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean.class);

            String filename = "/tmp/heapdump-" + System.currentTimeMillis() + ".hprof";
            mxBean.dumpHeap(filename, true);

            log.info("Heap dump generated: {}", filename);
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Failed to generate heap dump", e);
        }
    }
}
```

**Ngày 246-249: Complete Deployment & Troubleshooting Guides**

## **📋 COMPLETE DEPLOYMENT GUIDE**

### **1. Step-by-Step Production Deployment**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# deploy.sh - Complete deployment script

set -e  # Exit on any error

# Configuration
APP_NAME="cuonghoang-dev"
VERSION=${1:-latest}
ENVIRONMENT=${2:-production}
NAMESPACE="cuonghoang-dev"

echo "🚀 Starting deployment of $APP_NAME version $VERSION to $ENVIRONMENT"

# 1. Pre-deployment checks
echo "📋 Running pre-deployment checks..."

# Check if kubectl is configured
if ! kubectl cluster-info &> /dev/null; then
    echo "❌ kubectl is not configured or cluster is not accessible"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if namespace exists
if ! kubectl get namespace $NAMESPACE &> /dev/null; then
    echo "📦 Creating namespace $NAMESPACE"
    kubectl create namespace $NAMESPACE
fi

# Check if required secrets exist
if ! kubectl get secret app-secrets -n $NAMESPACE &> /dev/null; then
    echo "❌ Required secrets not found. Please create app-secrets first."
    exit 1
fi

# 2. Database migration
echo "🗄️ Running database migrations..."
kubectl apply -f k8s/migration-job.yaml -n $NAMESPACE

# Wait for migration to complete
kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/db-migration -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=300s

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Database migration failed"
    exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Database migration completed"

# 3. Build and push Docker image
echo "🐳 Building and pushing Docker image..."
docker build -t $APP_NAME:$VERSION .
docker tag $APP_NAME:$VERSION your-registry.com/$APP_NAME:$VERSION
docker push your-registry.com/$APP_NAME:$VERSION

echo "✅ Docker image pushed successfully"

# 4. Update Kubernetes manifests
echo "📝 Updating Kubernetes manifests..."
sed -i "s|image: .*|image: your-registry.com/$APP_NAME:$VERSION|g" k8s/app-deployment.yaml

# 5. Deploy to Kubernetes
echo "☸️ Deploying to Kubernetes..."

# Apply ConfigMaps and Secrets first
kubectl apply -f k8s/configmap.yaml -n $NAMESPACE
kubectl apply -f k8s/secret.yaml -n $NAMESPACE

# Deploy database and Redis
kubectl apply -f k8s/mysql-deployment.yaml -n $NAMESPACE
kubectl apply -f k8s/redis-deployment.yaml -n $NAMESPACE

# Wait for database to be ready
echo "⏳ Waiting for database to be ready..."
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=mysql -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=300s

# Deploy application
kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml -n $NAMESPACE
kubectl apply -f k8s/app-service.yaml -n $NAMESPACE
kubectl apply -f k8s/ingress.yaml -n $NAMESPACE

# 6. Wait for deployment to complete
echo "⏳ Waiting for deployment to complete..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=600s

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Deployment failed"
    echo "🔄 Rolling back to previous version..."
    kubectl rollout undo deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE
    exit 1
fi

# 7. Health check
echo "🏥 Running health checks..."
sleep 30  # Wait for application to start

# Get service endpoint
SERVICE_IP=$(kubectl get service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}')

if [ -z "$SERVICE_IP" ]; then
    SERVICE_IP=$(kubectl get service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}')
fi

# Health check
HEALTH_CHECK_URL="http://$SERVICE_IP:80/actuator/health"
for i in {1..10}; do
    if curl -f $HEALTH_CHECK_URL &> /dev/null; then
        echo "✅ Health check passed"
        break
    else
        echo "⏳ Health check attempt $i/10 failed, retrying..."
        sleep 10
    fi

    if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
        echo "❌ Health check failed after 10 attempts"
        exit 1
    fi
done

# 8. Smoke tests
echo "🧪 Running smoke tests..."
./scripts/smoke-tests.sh $SERVICE_IP

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Smoke tests failed"
    exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully!"
echo "🌐 Application is available at: https://cuonghoang.dev"

# 9. Post-deployment tasks
echo "📊 Running post-deployment tasks..."

# Update monitoring dashboards
kubectl apply -f monitoring/grafana-dashboards.yaml

# Send deployment notification
curl -X POST "https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/SLACK/WEBHOOK" \
     -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
     --data "{\"text\":\"✅ $APP_NAME version $VERSION deployed successfully to $ENVIRONMENT\"}"

echo "🎉 Deployment process completed!"
```

### **2. Blue-Green Deployment Strategy**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# blue-green-deploy.sh

APP_NAME="cuonghoang-dev"
NEW_VERSION=$1
NAMESPACE="cuonghoang-dev"

# Determine current and new environments
CURRENT_ENV=$(kubectl get service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.version}')
if [ "$CURRENT_ENV" = "blue" ]; then
    NEW_ENV="green"
else
    NEW_ENV="blue"
fi

echo "🔄 Starting blue-green deployment..."
echo "📍 Current environment: $CURRENT_ENV"
echo "🎯 Target environment: $NEW_ENV"

# 1. Deploy to new environment
echo "🚀 Deploying version $NEW_VERSION to $NEW_ENV environment..."

# Update deployment manifest
sed -i "s|version: .*|version: $NEW_ENV|g" k8s/app-deployment-$NEW_ENV.yaml
sed -i "s|image: .*|image: your-registry.com/$APP_NAME:$NEW_VERSION|g" k8s/app-deployment-$NEW_ENV.yaml

# Deploy to new environment
kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment-$NEW_ENV.yaml -n $NAMESPACE

# Wait for new deployment to be ready
kubectl rollout status deployment/$APP_NAME-$NEW_ENV -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=600s

# 2. Health check on new environment
echo "🏥 Running health checks on $NEW_ENV environment..."
NEW_SERVICE_IP=$(kubectl get service $APP_NAME-service-$NEW_ENV -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}')

for i in {1..10}; do
    if curl -f "http://$NEW_SERVICE_IP:80/actuator/health" &> /dev/null; then
        echo "✅ Health check passed on $NEW_ENV"
        break
    else
        echo "⏳ Health check attempt $i/10 failed, retrying..."
        sleep 10
    fi

    if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
        echo "❌ Health check failed on $NEW_ENV environment"
        exit 1
    fi
done

# 3. Run smoke tests on new environment
echo "🧪 Running smoke tests on $NEW_ENV environment..."
./scripts/smoke-tests.sh $NEW_SERVICE_IP

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Smoke tests failed on $NEW_ENV environment"
    exit 1
fi

# 4. Switch traffic to new environment
echo "🔀 Switching traffic to $NEW_ENV environment..."
kubectl patch service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"version":"'$NEW_ENV'"}}}'

# 5. Monitor for issues
echo "📊 Monitoring new environment for 5 minutes..."
sleep 300

# Check error rates and response times
ERROR_RATE=$(curl -s "http://prometheus:9090/api/v1/query?query=rate(http_requests_total{status=~\"5..\"}[5m])" | jq -r '.data.result[0].value[1]')

if (( $(echo "$ERROR_RATE > 0.01" | bc -l) )); then
    echo "❌ High error rate detected: $ERROR_RATE"
    echo "🔄 Rolling back to $CURRENT_ENV environment..."
    kubectl patch service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"version":"'$CURRENT_ENV'"}}}'
    exit 1
fi

# 6. Cleanup old environment
echo "🧹 Cleaning up $CURRENT_ENV environment..."
kubectl delete deployment $APP_NAME-$CURRENT_ENV -n $NAMESPACE

echo "✅ Blue-green deployment completed successfully!"
```

### **3. Rollback Strategy**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# rollback.sh

APP_NAME="cuonghoang-dev"
NAMESPACE="cuonghoang-dev"
ROLLBACK_REVISION=${1:-0}  # 0 means previous revision

echo "🔄 Starting rollback process..."

# 1. Check current deployment status
echo "📊 Current deployment status:"
kubectl rollout history deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE

# 2. Perform rollback
if [ "$ROLLBACK_REVISION" = "0" ]; then
    echo "⏪ Rolling back to previous revision..."
    kubectl rollout undo deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE
else
    echo "⏪ Rolling back to revision $ROLLBACK_REVISION..."
    kubectl rollout undo deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE --to-revision=$ROLLBACK_REVISION
fi

# 3. Wait for rollback to complete
echo "⏳ Waiting for rollback to complete..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/$APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE --timeout=300s

# 4. Verify rollback
echo "✅ Rollback completed. New status:"
kubectl get deployment $APP_NAME -n $NAMESPACE

# 5. Health check after rollback
echo "🏥 Running health check after rollback..."
SERVICE_IP=$(kubectl get service $APP_NAME-service -n $NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{.spec.clusterIP}')

for i in {1..5}; do
    if curl -f "http://$SERVICE_IP:80/actuator/health" &> /dev/null; then
        echo "✅ Health check passed after rollback"
        break
    else
        echo "⏳ Health check attempt $i/5 failed, retrying..."
        sleep 10
    fi

    if [ $i -eq 5 ]; then
        echo "❌ Health check failed after rollback"
        exit 1
    fi
done

echo "🎉 Rollback completed successfully!"
```

jstack <pid>           # Thread dump

perf top -p <pid>      # CPU profiling

# 4. Database Performance

SHOW FULL PROCESSLIST;

# Analyze query performance
EXPLAIN EXTENDED SELECT * FROM blogs WHERE category_id = 1;

# Check index usage
SHOW INDEX FROM blogs;

### **2. Memory Leak Detection & Resolution**
```java
// Memory Leak Troubleshooting Service
@Service
public class MemoryLeakTroubleshooter {

    private final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MemoryLeakTroubleshooter.class);

    @Scheduled(fixedRate = 60000) // Every minute
    public void analyzeMemoryUsage() {
        MemoryMXBean memoryBean = ManagementFactory.getMemoryMXBean();
        MemoryUsage heapUsage = memoryBean.getHeapMemoryUsage();

        long used = heapUsage.getUsed();
        long max = heapUsage.getMax();
        double usagePercentage = (double) used / max * 100;

        log.info("Heap Memory Usage: {}MB / {}MB ({}%)",
            used / 1024 / 1024, max / 1024 / 1024, String.format("%.2f", usagePercentage));

        if (usagePercentage > 85) {
            log.warn("High memory usage detected: {}%", String.format("%.2f", usagePercentage));
            analyzeMemoryLeaks();
        }
    }

    private void analyzeMemoryLeaks() {
        // 1. Check for common memory leak patterns
        checkForUnclosedResources();
        checkForStaticCollections();
        checkForListeners();

        // 2. Generate heap dump for analysis
        generateHeapDump();

        // 3. Force garbage collection
        System.gc();

        // 4. Clear caches if necessary
        clearCachesIfNecessary();
    }

    private void checkForUnclosedResources() {
        // Monitor file descriptors
        try {
            OperatingSystemMXBean osBean = ManagementFactory.getOperatingSystemMXBean();
            if (osBean instanceof UnixOperatingSystemMXBean) {
                UnixOperatingSystemMXBean unixBean = (UnixOperatingSystemMXBean) osBean;
                long openFileDescriptors = unixBean.getOpenFileDescriptorCount();
                long maxFileDescriptors = unixBean.getMaxFileDescriptorCount();

                double fdUsage = (double) openFileDescriptors / maxFileDescriptors * 100;

                if (fdUsage > 80) {
                    log.warn("High file descriptor usage: {} / {} ({}%)",
                        openFileDescriptors, maxFileDescriptors, String.format("%.2f", fdUsage));
                }
            }
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("Error checking file descriptors", e);
        }
    }

    private void checkForStaticCollections() {
        // This would require custom implementation based on your static collections
        log.info("Checking static collections for memory leaks...");

        // Example: Check cache sizes
        CacheManager cacheManager = getCacheManager();
        cacheManager.getCacheNames().forEach(cacheName -> {
            Cache cache = cacheManager.getCache(cacheName);
            if (cache instanceof CaffeineCache) {
                com.github.benmanes.caffeine.cache.Cache<Object, Object> nativeCache =
                    ((CaffeineCache) cache).getNativeCache();

                long size = nativeCache.estimatedSize();
                log.info("Cache '{}' size: {}", cacheName, size);

                if (size > 10000) {
                    log.warn("Large cache detected: '{}' with {} entries", cacheName, size);
                }
            }
        });
    }

    private void checkForListeners() {
        // Check for event listeners that might not be properly removed
        log.info("Checking for potential listener leaks...");

        // This is application-specific - check your event listeners
        ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher = getEventPublisher();
        // Custom logic to check listener counts
    }
}
```

### **3. Database Connection Issues**
```java
// Database Troubleshooting Service
@Service
public class DatabaseTroubleshooter {

    @Autowired
    private DataSource dataSource;

    public DatabaseHealthReport generateHealthReport() {
        DatabaseHealthReport report = new DatabaseHealthReport();

        try {
            // 1. Test basic connectivity
            try (Connection conn = dataSource.getConnection()) {
                report.setConnectionAvailable(true);
                report.setConnectionTime(measureConnectionTime());

                // 2. Check database version
                DatabaseMetaData metaData = conn.getMetaData();
                report.setDatabaseVersion(metaData.getDatabaseProductVersion());

                // 3. Check connection pool status
                if (dataSource instanceof HikariDataSource) {
                    HikariDataSource hikariDS = (HikariDataSource) dataSource;
                    HikariPoolMXBean poolBean = hikariDS.getHikariPoolMXBean();

                    report.setActiveConnections(poolBean.getActiveConnections());
                    report.setIdleConnections(poolBean.getIdleConnections());
                    report.setTotalConnections(poolBean.getTotalConnections());
                    report.setThreadsAwaitingConnection(poolBean.getThreadsAwaitingConnection());
                }

                // 4. Test query performance
                long queryTime = measureQueryPerformance(conn);
                report.setQueryPerformance(queryTime);

                // 5. Check for locks
                List<String> locks = checkForLocks(conn);
                report.setActiveLocks(locks);

            }
        } catch (SQLException e) {
            report.setConnectionAvailable(false);
            report.setError(e.getMessage());
        }

        return report;
    }

    private long measureConnectionTime() {
        long startTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
        try (Connection conn = dataSource.getConnection()) {
            return System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime;
        } catch (SQLException e) {
            return -1;
        }
    }

    private long measureQueryPerformance(Connection conn) throws SQLException {
        String testQuery = "SELECT 1";
        long startTime = System.currentTimeMillis();

        try (PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(testQuery);
             ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery()) {
            rs.next();
            return System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime;
        }
    }

    private List<String> checkForLocks(Connection conn) throws SQLException {
        String lockQuery = """
            SELECT
                r.trx_id waiting_trx_id,
                r.trx_mysql_thread_id waiting_thread,
                r.trx_query waiting_query,
                b.trx_id blocking_trx_id,
                b.trx_mysql_thread_id blocking_thread,
                b.trx_query blocking_query
            FROM information_schema.innodb_lock_waits w
            INNER JOIN information_schema.innodb_trx b ON b.trx_id = w.blocking_trx_id
            INNER JOIN information_schema.innodb_trx r ON r.trx_id = w.requesting_trx_id
            """;

        List<String> locks = new ArrayList<>();
        try (PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(lockQuery);
             ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery()) {

            while (rs.next()) {
                String lockInfo = String.format(
                    "Waiting TRX: %s (Thread: %s) blocked by TRX: %s (Thread: %s)",
                    rs.getString("waiting_trx_id"),
                    rs.getString("waiting_thread"),
                    rs.getString("blocking_trx_id"),
                    rs.getString("blocking_thread")
                );
                locks.add(lockInfo);
            }
        }

        return locks;
    }
}
```

### **4. Network & Connectivity Issues**
```bash
# Network Troubleshooting Commands

# 1. Check pod connectivity
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- ping <target-ip>
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- nslookup <service-name>
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- telnet <service-name> <port>

# 2. Check service endpoints
kubectl get endpoints <service-name> -n <namespace>
kubectl describe service <service-name> -n <namespace>

# 3. Check network policies
kubectl get networkpolicies -n <namespace>
kubectl describe networkpolicy <policy-name> -n <namespace>

# 4. Check ingress configuration
kubectl get ingress -n <namespace>
kubectl describe ingress <ingress-name> -n <namespace>

# 5. Check DNS resolution
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- nslookup kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local
kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- cat /etc/resolv.conf

# 6. Port forwarding for debugging
kubectl port-forward pod/<pod-name> 8080:8080
kubectl port-forward service/<service-name> 8080:80
```

### **5. Log Analysis & Debugging**
```bash
# Log Analysis Scripts

# 1. Analyze application logs
#!/bin/bash
# analyze-logs.sh

LOG_FILE=${1:-/var/log/application.log}
TIME_RANGE=${2:-"1 hour ago"}

echo "📊 Analyzing logs from $TIME_RANGE..."

# Error analysis
echo "🔴 Error Summary:"
grep -i "error\|exception\|failed" $LOG_FILE | \
    awk '{print $1, $2, $3}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -10

# Response time analysis
echo "⏱️ Slow Requests (>1000ms):"
grep "Request processed in" $LOG_FILE | \
    awk '{print $(NF-1)}' | sed 's/ms//' | \
    awk '$1 > 1000 {print $1}' | sort -nr | head -10

# Memory warnings
echo "💾 Memory Warnings:"
grep -i "memory\|heap\|gc" $LOG_FILE | tail -20

# Database connection issues
echo "🗄️ Database Issues:"
grep -i "connection\|timeout\|deadlock" $LOG_FILE | tail -10

# 2. Real-time log monitoring
tail -f $LOG_FILE | while read line; do
    if echo "$line" | grep -qi "error\|exception"; then
        echo "🚨 $(date): $line"
    elif echo "$line" | grep -qi "warn"; then
        echo "⚠️ $(date): $line"
    fi
done
```

### **6. Performance Bottleneck Identification**
```java
// Performance Bottleneck Detector
@Component
public class PerformanceBottleneckDetector {

    private final MeterRegistry meterRegistry;
    private final Map<String, Timer.Sample> activeSamples = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();

    @EventListener
    public void handleRequestStart(RequestStartEvent event) {
        Timer.Sample sample = Timer.start(meterRegistry);
        activeSamples.put(event.getRequestId(), sample);
    }

    @EventListener
    public void handleRequestEnd(RequestEndEvent event) {
        Timer.Sample sample = activeSamples.remove(event.getRequestId());
        if (sample != null) {
            Timer timer = Timer.builder("http.request.duration")
                .tag("endpoint", event.getEndpoint())
                .tag("method", event.getMethod())
                .register(meterRegistry);

            long duration = sample.stop(timer);

            // Alert on slow requests
            if (duration > 2000) { // 2 seconds
                log.warn("Slow request detected: {} {} took {}ms",
                    event.getMethod(), event.getEndpoint(), duration);

                analyzeSlowRequest(event, duration);
            }
        }
    }

    private void analyzeSlowRequest(RequestEndEvent event, long duration) {
        // 1. Check database query times
        checkDatabasePerformance(event.getRequestId());

        // 2. Check external service calls
        checkExternalServiceCalls(event.getRequestId());

        // 3. Check memory usage during request
        checkMemoryUsage();

        // 4. Check thread pool status
        checkThreadPoolStatus();
    }

    @Scheduled(fixedRate = 300000) // Every 5 minutes
    public void detectBottlenecks() {
        // 1. Analyze response time trends
        analyzeResponseTimeTrends();

        // 2. Check resource utilization
        checkResourceUtilization();

        // 3. Identify slow endpoints
        identifySlowEndpoints();

        // 4. Check for memory leaks
        checkForMemoryLeaks();
    }

    private void analyzeResponseTimeTrends() {
        // Get 95th percentile response times for last hour
        Timer timer = meterRegistry.find("http.request.duration").timer();
        if (timer != null) {
            double p95 = timer.takeSnapshot().percentileValue(0.95);

            if (p95 > 1000) { // 1 second
                log.warn("High 95th percentile response time: {}ms", p95);

                // Trigger detailed analysis
                generatePerformanceReport();
            }
        }
    }

    private void generatePerformanceReport() {
        PerformanceReport report = PerformanceReport.builder()
            .timestamp(LocalDateTime.now())
            .memoryUsage(getMemoryUsage())
            .cpuUsage(getCpuUsage())
            .threadPoolStatus(getThreadPoolStatus())
            .databaseConnectionPool(getDatabaseConnectionPoolStatus())
            .slowestEndpoints(getSlowestEndpoints())
            .build();

        // Save report and send alerts
        savePerformanceReport(report);
        sendPerformanceAlert(report);
    }
}
```
